case_title: FISHER INVESTMENTS, INC. VS. JOSEPH P. HALL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF COUNSEL IN OPPOSITION TO CONFIRMATION OF ARBITRATION AWARD AS REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF FISHER INVESTMENT, INC. FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P. SECTION 1285.2 AND FOR CONFIRMATION OF THE ENTIRE AWARD AS ISSUED BY THE ARBITRATOR AFTER TRIAL PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 1285.6 (TRANSACTION ID # 61264893) FILED BY RESPONDENT HALL, JOSEPH P.
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES (TRANSACTION ID # 61264752) FILED BY RESPONDENT HALL, JOSEPH P.

DECLARATION OF COUNSEL IN OPPOSITION TO CONFIRMATION OF ARBITRATION AWARD AS REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF FISHER INVESTMENT INC FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP SECTION 12852 AND FOR CONFIRMATION OF THE ENTIRE AWARD AS ISSUED BY THE ARBITRATOR AFTER TRAIL PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 12856 (TRANSACTION ID # 61264752) FILED BY RESPONDENT HALL, JOSEPH P.
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 100025485) FILED BY PETITIONER FISHER INVESTMENTS, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER FISHER INVESTMENTS, INC.'S PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. THE PETITION IMPERMISSIBLY: 1) SEEKS CONFIRMATION OF ONLY A PORTION OF THE AWARD, 2) SEEKS AN ORDER THAT EXCEEDS THE AWARD MADE BY THE ARBITRATOR; AND 3) FAILS TO FILE THE ENTIRE UNREDACTED DECISION AND AWARD OF THE ARBITRATOR. IF FISHER BELIEVES THAT THERE IS GOOD CAUSE FOR THE SEALING OF ANY DOCUMENTS, IN ANY FUTURE FILINGS FISHER MAY WISH TO FILE A MOTION TO SEAL AND FILE THE ASSERTEDLY CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENTS CONDITIONALLY UNDER SEAL. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Scott L. Metzger of the law offices of Duckor Sprading Metzger & Wynne (619-209-3000) appears by telephone as counsel for petitioner

Attorney, Richard S. Miller of the law offices of Richard S. Miller, Esq. (925-926-0500) appears as counsel for respondent/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PETITIONER FISHER INVESTMENTS, INC.'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is DENIED without prejudice to being refiled. The petition impermissibly: 1) seeks confirmation of only a portion of the award, 2) seeks an order that exceeds the award made by the arbitrator; and 3) fails to file the entire unredacted decision and award of the arbitrator. If Fisher believes that there is good cause for the sealing of any documents, in any future filings Fisher may wish to file a motion to seal and file the assertedly confidential documents conditionally under seal. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
